NSW-PARCEL-001.carbon_detail: Moderate-to-strong soil organic carbon potential based on public national soil-carbon mapping.
NSW-PARCEL-001.soil_detail: Stable upland soils with moderate erosion sensitivity indicated in public soil grid products.
NSW-PARCEL-001.water_detail: Seasonal drainage influence and limited riparian reach in nearby public hydrology layers.
NSW-PARCEL-001.biodiversity_detail: Remnant woodland intersects public biodiversity significance grids at a moderate level.

NSW-PARCEL-002.carbon_detail: Carbon opportunity is moderate; terrain and existing woody cover suggest sequestration potential.
NSW-PARCEL-002.soil_detail: Steeper terrain lowers soil resilience and raises restoration complexity.
NSW-PARCEL-002.water_detail: Less direct waterway exposure than other parcels in this prototype catchment.
NSW-PARCEL-002.biodiversity_detail: Strong overlap with publicly available species and vegetation significance layers.

NSW-PARCEL-003.carbon_detail: Alluvial soils show moderate carbon opportunity in public soil-organic-carbon datasets.
NSW-PARCEL-003.soil_detail: Fertile flats with stronger soil function but some compaction risk.
NSW-PARCEL-003.water_detail: High riparian relevance due to creek adjacency and catchment proximity.
NSW-PARCEL-003.biodiversity_detail: Biodiversity value is present but more fragmented than the escarpment parcel.
